evals.prompt: quiz me on what I just read — I finished a chapter about the Default Mode Network in a neuroscience book. It was maybe 20 pages.
evals.expected_output: Claude runs a full SQ3R session: asks for a survey/overview, generates 5-7 questions at surface/applied/synthesis levels, evaluates simulated user answers honestly (pushing back on vague ones), and ends with a confidence score + what to revisit.

assertions.name: has_survey_phase
assertions.description: Session begins with a survey/overview request before questions are asked

assertions.name: has_5_to_7_questions
assertions.description: Between 5 and 7 questions are generated

assertions.name: has_depth_levels
assertions.description: Questions are organized or labeled by depth (surface/applied/synthesis or Level 1/2/3)

assertions.name: pushes_back_on_vague
assertions.description: When user gives a vague answer, Claude explicitly pushes back and asks for more specificity before providing the answer

assertions.name: has_confidence_score
assertions.description: A numerical confidence score (X/10) is given in the review phase

assertions.name: has_revisit_list
assertions.description: Specific items to revisit are listed, not just a general summary

assertions.name: has_consolidation_prompt
assertions.description: A closing reflection or consolidation prompt is offered

evals.prompt: SQ3R me on habit loops. I just finished chapter 3 of Atomic Habits by James Clear.
evals.expected_output: Claude correctly identifies SQ3R trigger, runs the structured session for habit loops: cue-craving-response-reward framework, questions cover both recall and application to user's life, synthesis question connects to behavior design or AI product work.

assertions.name: has_survey_phase
assertions.description: Session begins with a survey/overview request before questions are asked

assertions.name: has_5_to_7_questions
assertions.description: Between 5 and 7 questions are generated

assertions.name: has_depth_levels
assertions.description: Questions are organized or labeled by depth (surface/applied/synthesis or Level 1/2/3)

assertions.name: pushes_back_on_vague
assertions.description: When user gives a vague answer, Claude explicitly pushes back and asks for more specificity before providing the answer

assertions.name: has_confidence_score
assertions.description: A numerical confidence score (X/10) is given in the review phase

assertions.name: has_revisit_list
assertions.description: Specific items to revisit are listed, not just a general summary

assertions.name: synthesis_question_applied
assertions.description: At least one synthesis question connects the topic to AI, product design, or behavior change (user's domain)
assertions.note: Only reliable when 'domains' is configured in SKILL.md frontmatter; without it, Claude infers domains from conversation context and results will vary

evals.prompt: can you test me on what I learned today? I watched a 45-minute video on retrieval practice vs spaced repetition — how they work, the research behind them, and when to use each.
evals.expected_output: Claude asks for topic clarification if needed (already given), runs survey phase, generates questions that distinguish retrieval practice from spaced repetition clearly, pushes back when user gives vague answers, ends with actionable revisit list.

assertions.name: has_survey_phase
assertions.description: Session begins with a survey/overview request before questions are asked

assertions.name: has_5_to_7_questions
assertions.description: Between 5 and 7 questions are generated

assertions.name: has_depth_levels

assertions.name: pushes_back_on_vague
assertions.description: When user gives a vague answer, Claude explicitly pushes back and asks for more specificity before providing the answer

assertions.name: has_confidence_score

assertions.description: Specific items to revisit are listed, not just a general summary

assertions.description: A closing reflection or consolidation prompt is offered
